content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES
COMMISSION
September 9, 1997
Sheila Lodge
1303 Las Alturas Road
Santa Barbara, California 93 103-1609
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-431
Dear Ms. Lodge:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
QUESTION
Is it a contribution to your campaign if a friend of yours provides graphics work for your
campaign, i.e., design a logo and the layouts for envelopes, letterhead, etc.?
CONCLUSION
Your friend may volunteer his personal time and expertise in the making of campaign
logos and layouts without making a contribution to your campaign. AU of the expenditures
incurred by your friend while working on your campaign must be reported as a non-monetary
contribution to your campaign. However, de minimus costs necessary to provide the graphics
work need not be reported.
FACTS
You are a candidate for the Santa Barbara City Council. Your friend has volunteered to
do the graphics for your campaign, including the design of a logo and the layout for envelopes,
letterhead, etc.
Government Code sections 81000
- 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
J:\97lttrs\9743 I .wpd
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No. A-97-43 1
Page 2
ANALYSIS
Section 82015 and Regulation 18215 state that the term “contribution” does not include
volunteer personal services. Therefore, your friend may volunteer his personal time and
expertise in the making of campaign logos and layouts without making a contribution to your
campaign.
However, any out-of-pocket expenses incurred by your friend while working on your
campaign must be reported as a non-monetary contribution to your campaign. (Thompson
Advice Letter, No. A-86-l21.) This excludes any de minimus costs necessary to provide the
graphics work need not be reported. (Lavagetto Advice Letter, No. 1-96-050.)
For example, if your ffiend spends $25 on supplies needed to produce the logo, the $25
would be a contribution. However, if your friend uses his own equipment and supplies on hand
at little or no cost, then the cost would be de minimus and no contribution would result.
Ifyou have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Marte Castafios
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:MC:jlw
CORRECTED LETTER: 09/15/97
(Page 2, deleted “you” from paragraph 3, sentence 1)
J:\97lttrs\9743 1 .wpd
